goal: Correct the previous ledger sticky-summary regression so row identity remains visible during horizontal scrolling without turning each row into an oversized card.
deliverable: A compact sticky context cell for the ledger
Supplier and purchase-link fields restored as independent columns
Normal table density restored so multiple rows remain visible in the common desktop viewport
Verification that the current baseline remains green
constraints: Keep the ledger as a table rather than a card layout
Preserve inline editing for the compact context inputs and existing supplier/link controls
Avoid backend contract or schema changes
Keep the round bounded to remediation of this ledger regression
acceptance_criteria: The sticky left-side context still identifies rows while scrolling right
The regressed oversized row layout is removed
Supplier and purchase-link workflows remain available as separate columns
py -3 scripts/validate_project.py passes after the remediation
product_acceptance_criteria: The ledger feels dense and scan-friendly again
The corrected sticky context keeps usefulness without overwhelming the row
manual_spot_checks: Open the ledger and confirm more than one row is visible in the common desktop viewport
Scroll to the right-side workflow columns and confirm the sticky context still identifies the row
Confirm supplier selection and purchase-link actions remain in dedicated columns
completion_language_policy: Only claim completion after the compact remediation ships and baseline validation reruns successfully.
delivery_truth_contract: Describe only the remediation that actually shipped, the governed artifacts, and the verification that ran.
inferred_assumptions: The user's priority is practical table density, not preserving the previous heavy summary implementation.
A compact two-line sticky context is the right midpoint between context retention and scan speed.
